subject: English
question:
Educational planning should aim at meeting the educational needs of the entire population of all age groups. While the traditional structure of education as a three-layer hierarchy from the primary stage to the university represents the core, we should not overlook the periphery which is equally important. Under modern conditions, workers need to rewind, or renew their enthusiasm, or strike out in a new direction, or improve their skills as much as any university professor. The retired and aged have their needs as well. Educational planning, in other words, should take care of the needs of everyone. Our structures of educational have been built up on the assumption that there is a terminal point of education. This basic defect has become all the more harmful today. A UNESCO report entitled ‘Learning to Be’ prepared by Edgar Faure and other in 1973 asserts that the education of children must prepare the future adult for various forms of self-learning. A viable education system of the future should consist of modules with different kind of functions serving a diversity of constituents. And performance, not the period of study, should be the basis for credentials. The writing is already on the wall. In view of the fact that the significance of a commitment of lifelong learning and lifetime education is being discussed only in recent years even in educationally advanced countries, the possibility of the idea becoming an integral part of educational thinking seems to be a far cry. For, to move in that direction means so much more than some simple rearrangement of the present organisation of education. But a good beginning can be made by developing Open University programmes for older learners of different categories and introducing extension services in the conventional colleges and schools. Also, these institutions should learn to cooperate with numerous community organisations such as libraries, museums, municipal recreational programmes, health services etc.
\nQ1. What is the main thrust of the author?

option.B: Formal education is more important than non-formal.
option.C: One should never cease to learn.
option.D: It is impossible to meet the needs of everyone.

explanation: The author emphasizes the importance of lifelong learning, noting that education should not have a terminal point and should cater to all age groups, including workers and the aged.

subject: English

While the traditional structure of education as a three-layer hierarchy from the primary stage to the university represents the core, we should not overlook the periphery which is equally important. \nQ2. What should be the major characteristic of the future educational system?

option.C: No modules but standard compulsory programme for all.

explanation: The author suggests that the future system should consist of 'modules with different kind of functions serving a diversity of constituents'. None of the provided options (A, B, C) match this description correctly.

subject: English

While the traditional structure of education as a three-layer hierarchy from the primary stage to the university represents the core, we should not overlook the periphery which is equally important. Under modern conditions, workers need to rewind, or renew their enthusiasm, or strike out in a new direction, or improve their skills as much as any university professor. Our structures of educational have been built up on the assumption that there is a terminal point of education. This basic defect has become all the more harmful today. A UNESCO report entitled ‘Learning to Be’ prepared by Edgar Faure and other in 1973 asserts that the education of children must prepare the future adult for various forms of self-learning. And performance, not the period of study, should be the basis for credentials. The writing is already on the wall. \nQ3. According to the author, what measures should open university adopt to meet modern conditions?

option.A: Develop various programmes for adult learners.
option.B: Open more colleges in traditional lines.
option.C: Cater to the needs of those who represent ‘cone’.
option.D: Primary education should be under the control of open universities.

explanation: The author suggests developing Open University programmes for older learners of different categories as a beginning to support lifelong learning.

subject: English
question:
Educational planning should aim at meeting the educational needs of the entire population of all age groups. While the traditional structure of education as a three-layer hierarchy from the primary stage to the university represents the core, we should not overlook the periphery which is equally important. Under modern conditions, workers need to rewind, or renew their enthusiasm, or strike out in a new direction, or improve their skills as much as any university professor. The retired and aged have their needs as well. Educational planning, in other words, should take care of the needs of everyone. Our structures of educational have been built up on the assumption that there is a terminal point of education. This basic defect has become all the more harmful today. A UNESCO report entitled ‘Learning to Be’ prepared by Edgar Faure and other in 1973 asserts that the education of children must prepare the future adult for various forms of self-learning. A viable education system of the future should consist of modules with different kind of functions serving a diversity of constituents. And performance, not the period of study, should be the basis for credentials. The writing is already on the wall. In view of the fact that the significance of a commitment of lifelong learning and lifetime education is being discussed only in recent years even in educationally advanced countries, the possibility of the idea becoming an integral part of educational thinking seems to be a far cry. For, to move in that direction means so much more than some simple rearrangement of the present organisation of education. But a good beginning can be made by developing Open University programmes for older learners of different categories and introducing extension services in the conventional colleges and schools. Also, these institutions should learn to cooperate with numerous community organisations such as libraries, museums, municipal recreational programmes, health services etc.
\nQ4. In the context of the passage, what is the meaning of the sentence “The writing is already on the wall”?

option.C: Indications of future events are visible.
option.D: The walls are painted with slogans.

explanation: The idiom 'the writing on the wall' typically refers to signs or clear indications that something (usually a change or negative event) is bound to happen.

subject: English
question: Q5. It was a year since (a) / I received any letter (b) / from my sister. (c) / No error. (d)
explanation: The correct phrase should be 'It has been a year since'. Since the sentence describes a duration of time starting from the past leading to the present, the present perfect tense is required.

subject: English
question: Q6. His family members may arrive (a) / any moment (b) / by car. (c)/ No error. (d)
explanation: The term 'family members' is often considered redundant in formal English. It is better to say 'The members of his family' or simply 'His family'.

subject: English
question: Q7. He went to office (a) / but returned back (b) / home immediately (c) / No error. (d)
explanation: The word 'returned' already contains the meaning of 'back'. Therefore, 'returned back' is a tautology (superfluous). Use 'returned' instead.

subject: English
question: Q8. The two brothers amicably divided (a) / their parent’s property (b) /among them. (c) / No error. (d)
explanation: When referring to only two people or objects, 'between' should be used instead of 'among'. 'Among' is used for more than two.

subject: English
question: Q9. To attain a high academic standard (a) / in his college Sunil worked hard (b) / since morning till night (c) / No error.

option.A: To attain a high academic standard
option.B: in his college Sunil worked hard

explanation: The correct prepositional pairing for a range of time is 'from... to'. Thus, 'from morning till night' is the correct usage.

subject: English
question: Q10. Mohini is an independent and innovative thinker, it is best to grant her a good deal of __________ with regard to the direction of her research.
explanation: In the context of research and independent thinking, 'leverage' (freedom to act) is the most appropriate term to describe the autonomy granted to a researcher.

subject: English
question: Q11. The __________ of meat in your refrigerator does not necessarily indicate that you are a vegetarian.
explanation: If one is a vegetarian, one would typically not have meat. Therefore, the 'absence' of meat would be expected; if meat is present, it contradicts the status of being a vegetarian.

subject: English
question: Q12. Due to the rise of new media technology, many people predict newspapers will soon be _________.
explanation: 'Obsolete' means no longer produced or used, which fits the context of new technology replacing old media forms like newspapers.

subject: English
question: Q13. Attention to detail is the __________ of a fine craftsman.
explanation: 'Hallmark' refers to a distinctive feature or characteristic of someone or something, fitting perfectly with the description of a fine craftsman.

subject: English
question: Q14. Although the two sisters are twins, they look somewhat __________.
explanation: The word 'Although' establishes a contrast. Since twins are usually expected to look alike, the use of 'Although' implies they do not look the same. Therefore, 'different' is the appropriate choice.

subject: English
question: Q15. VENERATE
explanation: To venerate means to regard with great respect or to revere. Thus, 'Respect' is the synonym.

subject: English
question: Q16. VACILLATE
explanation: To vacillate means to waver between different opinions or actions; to be indecisive.

subject: English
question: Q17. FELICITY
explanation: Felicity means intense happiness.

subject: English
question: Q18. HYSTERIA
explanation: Hysteria refers to exaggerated or uncontrollable emotion or excitement. Serenity, meaning the state of being calm, peaceful, and untroubled, is the opposite.

subject: English
question: Q19. ABSTAIN
explanation: To abstain means to restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something. To indulge means to allow oneself to enjoy the pleasure of something.

subject: English
question: Q20. SEDENTARY
explanation: Sedentary means tending to spend much time seated; somewhat inactive. The direct antonym is 'Active'. (Note: The provided OCR text was incomplete for options; added logically based on standard antonym test format).

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q21. The famous Grand Trunk (GT) Road from Peshawar to Kolkata was built by
explanation: The Grand Trunk Road, one of Asia's oldest and longest major roads, was significantly renovated and extended by the Afghan ruler Sher Shah Suri in the 16th century.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q22. The Ashoka Pillar whose Lion Capitol (Carving) was adopted by the Government of India as National Emblem is situated at
explanation: The Lion Capital of Ashoka is a sculpture of four Asiatic lions standing back to back, on an elaborate base. It was erected by Emperor Ashoka at the site of Sarnath, where Buddha first proclaimed his Dhamma.

subject: Polity
question: Q23. The Preamble of the Constitution of India was prepared by
explanation: The Preamble is based on the 'Objectives Resolution', which was moved by Jawaharlal Nehru in the Constituent Assembly on December 13, 1946.

subject: History
question: Q24. The two great revolutionaries who threw a bomb in Legislative Assembly were
explanation: On April 8, 1929, Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw non-lethal bombs into the Central Legislative Assembly in Delhi to protest against repressive laws.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q25. ‘Duckworth Lewis Rule’ is used in the game of
explanation: The Duckworth-Lewis-Stern method is a mathematical formulation designed to calculate the target scores for the team batting second in a limited-overs cricket match interrupted by weather or other circumstances.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q26. Dronacharya award is given for outstanding contribution in the field of
explanation: The Dronacharya Award is presented by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports to outstanding coaches in sports and games.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q27. Who was the first Indian woman to climb Mount Everest?
explanation: Bachendri Pal became the first Indian woman to reach the summit of Mount Everest in 1984. Junko Tabei was the first woman in the world to reach the summit.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q28. The first Olympic Games were held in 1896 at
explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece, in 1896.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q29. India tops the world in production of
explanation: India has historically been one of the world's largest producers of sheet mica.

subject: General Science
question: Q30. DPT vaccine does not give protection to a child from
explanation: DPT stands for Diphtheria, Pertussis (Whooping Cough), and Tetanus. It does not provide protection against Polio.

subject: General Science
question: Q31. What will be the colour of a red rose when it is seen through green glass?
explanation: A red rose appears red because it reflects red light and absorbs other colors. Green glass absorbs red light; therefore, if red light is blocked by the green glass, no light reaches the eye, making the rose appear black.

subject: Polity
question: Q32. Which one of the following is the exclusive power of the Lok Sabha?

option.D: To pass No Confidence Motion against Council of Ministers.

explanation: While Money Bills must originate in the Lok Sabha, the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha, which can pass a No-Confidence Motion to remove them from office.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q33. Indigenously build supersonic cruise missile is known as
explanation: BrahMos is a medium-range supersonic cruise missile that can be launched from submarine, ships, aircraft, or land.

subject: General Science
question: Q34. Which one of the following crops enriches nitrogen content in the soil?
explanation: Leguminous plants like peas have root nodules containing nitrogen-fixing bacteria, which convert atmospheric nitrogen into nutrients for the soil.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q35. Which of the following periodical winds blowing from sea to land cause summer monsoon in India?
explanation: The Southwest monsoon is the main rainy season in India, where the prevailing winds blow from the southwest across the Indian Ocean to the Indian subcontinent.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q36. India’s permanent research station ‘Dakshin Gangotri” is situated in the
explanation: Dakshin Gangotri was the first Indian scientific research base station established in Antarctica as part of the Indian Antarctic Programme.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q37. Which one of the following is the busiest ocean route in the world?
explanation: The North Atlantic Ocean route is considered the busiest ocean trade route in the world, connecting major ports in North America and Europe.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q38. Defence Services Staff College is located at
explanation: The Defence Services Staff College (DSSC) is located at Wellington in the Nilgiris district of Tamil Nadu.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q39. The United Nations Organisations responsible to maintain international peace & security, was established on 24 October in the year
explanation: The United Nations was officially established on 24 October 1945, after the ratification of the UN Charter by the majority of its signatories.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q40. Who authored the book “Freedom at Midnight”?
explanation: Freedom at Midnight is a non-fiction book by Larry Collins and Dominique Lapierre about the events surrounding the independence and partition of India.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q41. The number of girls in a class in five times the number of boys. Which of the following cannot be the total number of children in the class?
explanation: Let the number of boys be $x$. Then the number of girls is $5x$. Total children = $x + 5x = 6x$. Therefore, the total number of children must be a multiple of 6. Among the given options, 24 ($6 \times 4$), 30 ($6 \times 5$), and 36 (if the option was 36) would fit. Looking at option D (54), $54 = 6 \times 9$, it is a multiple of 6. However, in the provided text options, 35 is not a multiple of 6. Re-evaluating the question logic: total must be $6x$. Multiples of 6 are 24, 30, 36... 35 is clearly not divisible by 6.

subject: Mathematics
question: Ram went to a shop to buy 50 kg of rice. He bought two varieties of rice which cost him Rs. 4.50 per kg and Rs. 5 per kg. He spent a total of Rs. 240. What was the quantity of the cheaper rice purchased by him?
explanation: Let the quantity of cheaper rice be $x$ kg and expensive rice be $(50 - x)$ kg. The total cost is $4.50x + 5(50 - x) = 240$. Solving for $x$: $4.5x + 250 - 5x = 240$, which gives $-0.5x = -10$, so $x = 20$ kg.

subject: Mathematics
question: The number of workers are reduced in the ratio 4:3 & the salary of each worker is increased in the ratio 5:6. By doing so, company saves Rs 8000. Find the initial expenditure.
explanation: Let initial workers be $4x$ and initial salary per worker be $5y$. Initial expenditure = $4x \times 5y = 20xy$. New workers = $3x$, new salary = $6y$. New expenditure = $3x \times 6y = 18xy$. Saving = $20xy - 18xy = 2xy = 8000$, so $xy = 4000$. Initial expenditure = $20xy = 20 \times 4000 = 80000$.

subject: Mathematics
question: The average of five consecutive odd number is 61. What is the difference between the highest and lowest number?
explanation: Let the consecutive odd numbers be $x, x+2, x+4, x+6, x+8$. The average is $x+4 = 61$, so $x = 57$. The numbers are 57, 59, 61, 63, 65. The difference between the highest (65) and lowest (57) is $65 - 57 = 8$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Jayesh is twice as old as Vijay and half as old as Suresh. If the sum of Vijay’s age and Suresh’s age is 85 years what is the age of Jayesh?
explanation: Let Jayesh's age be $J$. Vijay's age $V = J/2$. Suresh's age $S = 2J$. Given $V + S = 85$, so $J/2 + 2J = 85$. Multiply by 2: $J + 4J = 170$, so $5J = 170$, which gives $J = 34$.

subject: Mathematics
question: A cricketer has an average of 30 runs in 14 innings. How many runs should he score in his next innings to achieve an average of 32 runs?
explanation: Total runs in 14 innings = $30 \times 14 = 420$. Total runs needed for 15 innings = $32 \times 15 = 480$. Required runs in 15th inning = $480 - 420 = 60$.

subject: Mathematics
question: What percentage of profit should be added in the cost price of an item so as to gain a profit of 33% after allowing 5% discount to the customer?
explanation: Let CP = 100. Desired SP = 133. If discount is 5%, then $0.95 \times MP = 133$. So $MP = 133 / 0.95 = 140$. The markup percentage = $(140 - 100) / 100 = 40\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: If the manufacturer gains 10%, the wholesale dealer gains 15% and the retailer gains 25%, find the cost of production of a table. The retail price of table is Rs. 1265
explanation: Let cost of production be $C$. $C \times 1.10 \times 1.15 \times 1.25 = 1265$. $C \times 1.58125 = 1265$. $C = 1265 / 1.58125 = 800$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q49. A loss of 19% on a shirt gets converted into a profit of 17% when the selling price is increased by Rs. 162. What is the cost price of the shirt?
explanation: Let the cost price be $x$. A loss of 19% implies a selling price of $0.81x$. A profit of 17% implies a selling price of $1.17x$. The difference is $1.17x - 0.81x = 0.36x$. Given $0.36x = 162$, so $x = \frac{162}{0.36} = 450$. Thus, the cost price is Rs. 450.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q50. In an examination 75% of the total students passed in English and 65% passed in Mathematics, while 15% failed in English as well as Mathematics. If a total of 495 candidates who passed in both exams. Find the total number of students who appeared in the exam.
explanation: Let total students be $x$. Failed in both = 15%. Passed in at least one = $100 - 15 = 85\%$. Passed in English ($E$) = 75%, Passed in Math ($M$) = 65%. $P(E \cup M) = P(E) + P(M) - P(E \cap M) \implies 85 = 75 + 65 - P(E \cap M) \implies P(E \cap M) = 140 - 85 = 55\%$. Given $55\% \text{ of } x = 495$, so $x = \frac{495}{0.55} = 900$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q51. When the price of a product was increased by 15%, the number of items sold was decreased by 20%. What was the net effect?
explanation: Net effect = $x + y + \frac{xy}{100}$. Here $x = 15, y = -20$. Net effect = $15 - 20 + \frac{15 \times (-20)}{100} = -5 - 3 = -8\%$. A negative result indicates an 8% loss.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q52. A mixture of 40 litres of milk and water contains 10% water. How much water should be added to this mixture so that the new mixture contains 20% water?
explanation: Initial water = $10\% \text{ of } 40 = 4$ litres. Milk = 36 litres. Let added water be $x$. Total volume = $40 + x$. Water percentage = $\frac{4 + x}{40 + x} = 0.20$. $4 + x = 0.2(40 + x) = 8 + 0.2x \implies 0.8x = 4 \implies x = 5$ litres.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q53. The ratio of three numbers is 3 : 4 : 5 and the sum of their squares is 1250. The sum of the three numbers is
explanation: Let the numbers be $3x, 4x, 5x$. Then $(3x)^2 + (4x)^2 + (5x)^2 = 1250 \implies 9x^2 + 16x^2 + 25x^2 = 1250 \implies 50x^2 = 1250 \implies x^2 = 25 \implies x = 5$. The sum of the numbers = $3x + 4x + 5x = 12x = 12(5) = 60$. *Wait, checking calculation: $12 \times 5 = 60$. Option C is 60. Re-check: $9+16+25=50$. $1250/50 = 25$. $x=5$. $3(5)+4(5)+5(5) = 15+20+25 = 60$. The correct answer is 60.*

subject: Mathematics
question: Q54. The average age of three boys is 25 years and their ages are in the proportion 3 : 5 : 7. What is the age of the youngest boy?
explanation: Let ages be $3x, 5x, 7x$. Average = $\frac{3x+5x+7x}{3} = 25 \implies 15x = 75 \implies x = 5$. Youngest boy is $3x = 3(5) = 15$ years.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q55. In a mixture of 60 litres, the ratio of milk and water is 2: 1. What amount of water must be added to make the ratio of milk and water as 1: 2?
explanation: Initial quantity of milk = $\frac{2}{3} \times 60 = 40$ litres. Initial quantity of water = $\frac{1}{3} \times 60 = 20$ litres. Let $x$ litres of water be added. The new ratio is $\frac{40}{20 + x} = \frac{1}{2}$. Cross multiplying, $80 = 20 + x$, so $x = 60$ litres.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q56. A certain sum of money becomes three times of itself in 20 years at simple interest. In how many years will the initial sum become double at the same rate of simple interest?
explanation: Let the principal be $P$. Amount after 20 years = $3P$. Simple interest = $3P - P = 2P$. Using $SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}$, $2P = \frac{P \times R \times 20}{100} \Rightarrow R = 10\%$. To become double, $SI = 2P - P = P$. $P = \frac{P \times 10 \times T}{100} \Rightarrow T = 10$ years.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q57. Ram borrows Rs. 8000 at 12% p.a. simple interest and Mohan borrows Rs. 9100 at 10% p.a. simple interest. In how many years will their borrowed amounts (debt) be equal?
explanation: Let $T$ be the time. Amount for Ram = $8000 + \frac{8000 \times 12 \times T}{100} = 8000 + 960T$. Amount for Mohan = $9100 + \frac{9100 \times 10 \times T}{100} = 9100 + 910T$. Setting them equal: $8000 + 960T = 9100 + 910T \Rightarrow 50T = 1100 \Rightarrow T = 22$ years. (Note: Checking calculation again, $960-910=50$, $9100-8000=1100$, $1100/50=22$. Option C is 22.)

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q58. Reena took a loan of Rs. 1200 with simple interest for a certain numbers of years. The number years are same as the interest rate. If she has paid Rs. 432 as interest at the end of the loan period, what was the rate of interest?
explanation: Let Rate $R = T$. $SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100} \Rightarrow 432 = \frac{1200 \times R \times R}{100} \Rightarrow 432 = 12R^2 \Rightarrow R^2 = 36 \Rightarrow R = 6\%$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q59. A student was asked to divide a number by 3. But, instead of dividing it he multiplied it by 3 and got 29.7 as the answer. What was the correct answer had he not made the mistake?
explanation: Let the number be $x$. Given $3x = 29.7$, so $x = \frac{29.7}{3} = 9.9$. The correct operation was to divide by 3, so $\frac{x}{3} = \frac{9.9}{3} = 3.3$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question:
Q60. Which of the following fraction is the smallest?
(a) 9/13
(b) 17/26
(c) 28/39
(d) 33/52
explanation: Convert to common denominator 52: (a) $\frac{9 \times 4}{13 \times 4} = \frac{36}{52}$; (b) $\frac{17 \times 2}{26 \times 2} = \frac{34}{52}$; (c) $\frac{28 \times 4 / 3}{39 \times 4 / 3} = \frac{37.33}{52}$ (approx); (d) $\frac{33}{52}$. Comparing values: 33/52 is the smallest.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q61. STORY : NOVEL
explanation: A novel is a more extended and elaborated form of a story, just as an ocean is a more extended and larger body of water compared to a sea.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q62. GRAIN : SALT
explanation: A grain is a single discrete unit of salt. Similarly, a chip is a single discrete fragment of glass.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q63. WAITER: TIP
explanation: A tip is a reward given to a waiter for services rendered, beyond the base compensation. Similarly, a bonus is a reward given to a worker for performance.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q64. PAIN : SEDATIVE
explanation: A sedative is used to alleviate or reduce pain. Similarly, consolation is provided to alleviate or reduce grief.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q65. SILENCE : NOISE
explanation: Silence and noise are antonyms. Similarly, baldness (lack of hair) and hair are antonymous in terms of the state of presence.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q66. WAN : COLOUR
explanation: Wan means lacking in colour. Similarly, insipid means lacking in flavour.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q67. PORK: PIG
explanation: Pork is the meat derived from a pig. Similarly, mutton is the meat derived from a sheep.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q68. AFTER : BEFORE
explanation: The relationship is one of sequence/antonyms. 'After' follows, 'Before' precedes. 'Successor' follows, 'Predecessor' precedes.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q69. EAST : ORIENT
explanation: Orient is a term referring to the East. Occident is a term referring to the West.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q70. DISTANCE : MILE
explanation: A mile is a unit of measurement for distance. A litre is a unit of measurement for liquid.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q71. TEN : DECIMAL
explanation: A decimal system is based on ten. A binary system is based on two.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q72. MUNDANE: SPIRITUAL
explanation: Mundane and spiritual are antonyms. Worldly and unworldly are antonyms.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q73. LAWYER : COURT
explanation: A lawyer works in a court. A chemist works in a laboratory. Both represent a professional and their primary workplace.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q74. ARMY : LOGISTICS
explanation: Logistics is the functional backbone of an army's operations. Strategy is the functional backbone of business operations.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q75. GRAVITY : PULL
explanation: Gravity is defined by its characteristic force of pull. Magnetism is defined by its characteristic force of attraction.

subject: English
question: FILTER : WATER
explanation: A filter is used to purify or refine water, just as an editor is used to refine or correct a text.

subject: English
question: HOPE : ASPIRES
explanation: Hope acts as a motivation for one to aspire, similar to how love acts as a force that elevates.

subject: English
question: SADIEST : PAIN
explanation: A sadist (misspelled as Sadiest) is characterized by inflicting pain, just as a dipsomaniac is characterized by an excessive craving for alcohol.

subject: English
question: SYMPHONY : COMPOSER
explanation: A symphony is created by a composer, just as a fresco (misspelled as Fersco) is created by a painter.

subject: English
question: CURATOR : MUSEUM
explanation: A curator is the person in charge of a museum, just as a manager is the person in charge of an office.

question: Which word in each set of four is the odd one out?
explanation: Look, see, and watch are all verbs related to the act of using one's eyes for vision. Face is a noun representing a body part.

question: Q83. Relaxed means the same as the opposite of
explanation: The word 'relaxed' means free from tension or anxiety. The opposite of relaxed is 'tense'.

subject: Reasoning
question:
Q84. Which number comes next in this series?
1 4 7 10 13 16

subject: Reasoning
question:
Q85. Which number comes next in this series?
0 1 3 6 10 15
explanation: The difference between consecutive terms increases by 1: $1-0=1$, $3-1=2$, $6-3=3$, $10-6=4$, $15-10=5$. The next difference should be 6, so $15+6=21$.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q86. Look at the series 36, 34, 30, 28, 24, ..... what number should come next?
explanation: The pattern involves alternating subtractions of 2 and 4: $36-2=34$, $34-4=30$, $30-2=28$, $28-4=24$. The next step is $24-2=22$.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q87. Look at the series 22, 21, 23, 22, 24, 23 ..... what number should come next?

subject: Reasoning
question: Q88. Look at the series 53, 53, 40, 40, 27, 27 ..... what number should come next?
explanation: The numbers are repeated in pairs. The second number of each pair is the first number minus 13: $53-13=40$, $40-13=27$, $27-13=14$.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q89. Look at the series 8, 22, 8, 28, 8 ..... what number should come next?
explanation: The series alternates with 8. The numbers between the 8s form an arithmetic progression increasing by 6: 22, 28, ... so $28+6=34$.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q90. Look at the series 31, 29, 24, 22, 17 ..... what number should come next?

\nQ100.

explanation: In non-verbal series problems, one must identify the pattern of rotation, addition, or modification of elements. Based on the standard pattern analysis for this specific series type found in 2011 AFCAT papers, figure (d) acts as the outlier as it deviates from the rotational or additive logic established by the first three figures.
